root_text.an6.83:1.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo aggaṁ dhammaṁ arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.83:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu assaddho hoti, ahiriko hoti, anottappī hoti, kusīto hoti, duppañño hoti, kāye ca jīvite ca sāpekkho hoti.
root_text.an6.83:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu abhabbo aggaṁ dhammaṁ arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.83:2.1: Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo aggaṁ dhammaṁ arahattaṁ sacchikātuṁ.
root_text.an6.83:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho hoti, hirīmā hoti, ottappī hoti, āraddhavīriyo hoti, paññavā hoti, kāye ca jīvite ca anapekkho hoti.
root_text.an6.83:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu bhabbo aggaṁ dhammaṁ arahattaṁ sacchikātun”ti.

translation_text.an6.83:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with six qualities can’t realize the best thing, perfection.
translation_text.an6.83:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is faithless, shameless, imprudent, lazy, and witless. And they’re concerned with their body and their life.
translation_text.an6.83:1.4: A mendicant with these six qualities can’t realize the best thing, perfection.
translation_text.an6.83:2.1: A mendicant with six qualities can realize the best thing, perfection.
translation_text.an6.83:2.3: It’s when a mendicant is faithful, conscientious, prudent, energetic, and wise. And they have no concern for their body and their life.
translation_text.an6.83:2.4: A mendicant with these six qualities can realize the best thing, perfection.”
